description: Create docs/EXPERIMENT.md with comprehensive experiment design documentation and update README.md to link to it prominently in the Documentation table and Table of Contents.
steps:
Step 1: Create docs/EXPERIMENT.md with the following sections structured as clear Markdown with proper headings:

## Overview & Goals
- This is a TDD Infrastructure-as-Code experiment exploring whether production-grade serverless infrastructure can be constructed entirely through strict issue-driven development with AI-assisted agents.
- Part of a broader experiment matrix: 5 programming languages x 3 AI assistants (15 repositories total).
- This specific repository: C# (.NET 8) with AWS CDK, driven by Kiro.
- Goal: build a fully functional event-driven sleep audio processing pipeline from zero to production-ready through pure TDD, with architecture documentation as the source of truth.

## Experiment Matrix & Actors
- The broader experiment uses 5 languages (C#, TypeScript, Python, Go, Java) and 3 AI agents.
- This repository's actor: Kiro (the C# flavor).
- The repository owner (obstreperous-ai) created issues, Kiro implemented them autonomously.
- Each AI agent receives the same set of issues (adapted for language) to enable cross-comparison.

## Methodology
### Strict TDD (Red-Green-Refactor)
- Every infrastructure resource follows the Red-Green-Refactor cycle.
- Tests use CDK Assertions (Template.FromStack, HasResourceProperties, ResourceCountIs) for millisecond feedback without AWS deployment.
- 148 total tests: 129 C# infrastructure tests + 19 Python Lambda unit tests.

### Issue-Driven Development
- The entire pipeline was built incrementally across 12 implementation issues (plus 2 documentation issues).
- Issues were numbered [1] through [14] with explicit ordering, dependencies, and acceptance criteria.
- Each issue produced a working, tested increment that could be deployed independently.

### Architecture-as-Code
- ARCHITECTURE.md written BEFORE any implementation code (Issue [2]).
- Architecture document served as the authoritative source of truth throughout development.
- All implementation issues traced back to components in the architecture.
- Mermaid diagrams maintained in sync with code throughout.

## Issue History & Development Timeline
Create a table summarizing all 14 issues with columns: Issue Number (internal sequence), GitHub Issue #, Title, PR #, Date Merged, Key Deliverable, Tests Added. Use the actual data from the issues:

| # | Issue | Title | PR | Merged | Key Deliverable | Tests |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | #1 | Bootstrap: C# CDK + Strict TDD + Agent Config | #2 | 2026-05-28 | xUnit test project, CI workflow, architecture skeleton | 2 |
| 2 | #3 | Architecture Design (Mermaid + Detailed Spec) | #4 | 2026-06-02 | Comprehensive ARCHITECTURE.md, AGENT_GUIDELINES.md | 0 (docs only) |
| 3 | #5 | Core S3 Buckets + EventBridge Rule | #6 | 2026-06-03 | Input/Output S3, EventBridge rule, stub SQS target | 12 |
| 4 | #7 | Step Functions State Machine + Polly | #8 | 2026-06-04 | State machine, Polly SDK integration, EventBridge wiring | 15 (total) |
| 5 | #9 | DynamoDB Metadata Table + State Machine I/O | #10 | 2026-06-05 | Metadata table, DynamoDB tasks in state machine | 24 (total) |
| 6 | #11 | SNS Notifications + Error Handling | #12 | 2026-06-06 | 2 SNS topics, error paths, status updates | 31 (total) |
| 7 | #13 | Lambda Function Skeleton + State Machine Integration | #14 | 2026-06-07 | Python 3.12 Lambda, LambdaInvoke task, DynamoDB access | 37 (total) |
| 8 | #15 | Complete Pipeline Wiring + Input Validation | #16 | 2026-06-08 | ValidateInput Choice state, Lambda validation, end-to-end flow | 45 (total) |
| 9 | #17 | Pipeline Testing + Multi-Environment + CDK Pipelines | #18 | 2026-06-09 | PipelineStack, env support, expanded test coverage | 62 (total) |
| 10 | #19 | Advanced Error Handling, Retries & Observability | #20 | 2026-06-10 | Retry policies, X-Ray, CloudWatch alarms/dashboard | 70 (total) |
| 11 | #21 | Full Audio Processing & Output Handling | #22 | 2026-06-11 | Lambda S3 read/write, processing pipeline, output metadata | 73 (total) |
| 12 | #23 | End-to-End Validation, Docs Polish & Completion | #24 | 2026-06-12 | 56 E2E tests, README rewrite, SUMMARY.md | 129 C# + 19 Python = 148 |
| 13 | #25 | Documentation: README Enrichment + Meta-Prompts | #26 | 2026-06-13 | META-PROMPTS.md, enriched README | 148 (unchanged) |
| 14 | #27 | Documentation: Experimental Design (this document) | - | - | EXPERIMENT.md | - |

## Prompting Patterns & Meta-Prompts
- Reference docs/META-PROMPTS.md for the full 6-pattern library.
- Summarize the key patterns used:
  1. Agent Instruction Template (AGENT_GUIDELINES.md)
  2. Issue-Driven Development (structured issues with acceptance criteria)
  3. TDD Infrastructure Pattern (CDK Assertions)
  4. Architecture-First Development (ARCHITECTURE.md as source of truth)
  5. Context Propagation (.agents/tasks/ structure)
  6. Error Handling and Observability (Step Functions patterns)
- Note how issues were structured with: Goal, Strict TDD Rules, Specific Requirements, Tasks (in strict order), Success Criteria, and 'Next Issue' pointers.

## Key Decisions & Trade-offs
- Step Functions over Lambda chaining (built-in retry, visual history, SDK integrations)
- Event-driven architecture (loose coupling, independent scaling, fault isolation)
- DynamoDB on-demand billing (bursty workloads, no capacity planning)
- Python Lambda with C# CDK (fast cold starts, rich SDK, separation of concerns)
- CustomState for Polly integration (no L2 construct available in CDK C#)
- Architecture documentation before any code (prevents drift, provides clear target)
- Single feature per issue with incremental complexity (each builds on the previous)

## Preliminary Observations
- TDD for infrastructure provides high confidence without deployment.
- CDK Assertions catch IAM, encryption, and configuration issues at synthesis time.
- Issue-driven development with explicit acceptance criteria enables autonomous agent implementation.
- Context propagation via task files prevents re-discovery of environment quirks across sessions.
- The architecture-first approach reduced implementation ambiguity significantly.
- JSII resource pressure was a recurring challenge with large test suites (resolved via serial execution).
- The project went from zero to 148 tests and ~25 AWS resources in 12 working days (May 28 - June 13, 2026).
- Strict TDD discipline was maintained throughout - no resource was added without a prior failing test.
- Maintaining architecture documentation alongside code prevented drift but required discipline.

## Final Metrics
- 148 automated tests (129 C# + 19 Python)
- ~25+ AWS resources in the main stack
- 12 implementation issues + 2 documentation issues
- 12 merged PRs (all by Kiro agent)
- Languages: C# (CDK), Python (Lambda)
- Security: KMS encryption on all data stores, least-privilege IAM, public access blocked
- Observability: CloudWatch Logs/Alarms/Dashboard, X-Ray tracing
- Development period: May 28 - June 13, 2026 (16 calendar days)

## Related Documentation
Link to other docs in the project: ARCHITECTURE.md, AGENT_GUIDELINES.md, META-PROMPTS.md, SUMMARY.md.

Step 2: Update README.md to add EXPERIMENT.md to the Documentation table (which currently has 4 entries: Architecture, Agent Guidelines, Project Summary, Meta-Prompts). Add a new row: | [Experiment Design](docs/EXPERIMENT.md) | Experimental methodology, issue history, and preliminary observations |

Step 3: Update the Table of Contents in README.md to include a link to a new 'Experiment Design' section or ensure the existing 'Experiment Methodology' section references EXPERIMENT.md for the full details.
Step 4: Commit all changes with a conventional commit message: docs: add EXPERIMENT.md capturing experimental design and methodology
acceptance_criteria: docs/EXPERIMENT.md exists with all required sections: Overview & Goals, Methodology, Actors & Setup, Prompting Patterns, Issue History Summary, Key Decisions & Trade-offs, Preliminary Observations
The issue history table in EXPERIMENT.md accurately reflects the 14 issues with correct GitHub issue numbers, PR numbers, and merge dates
README.md Documentation table includes a row linking to docs/EXPERIMENT.md
All markdown links in the new document resolve correctly (relative links to other docs/ files)
Changes are committed with a conventional commit message
verification: Read docs/EXPERIMENT.md and verify it contains all required sections
Read README.md and verify the Documentation table includes EXPERIMENT.md
Verify relative links in EXPERIMENT.md point to valid files (ARCHITECTURE.md, META-PROMPTS.md, AGENT_GUIDELINES.md, SUMMARY.md)
